title: Body impact protection system
abstract: A body impact protection system includes an inner layer and an impact force dampening and defusing structure. The inner layer includes a material composition and is adjacent to a body part when the body impact protection system is worn. The impact force dampening and defusing structure is juxtaposed to the inner layer and includes a plurality of components. The components function to reduce pressure on the body part from an impact force on a layer by layer basis. Each layer of the system dampens and defuses the impact force such that, by the time it reaches the body part, it has been substantially attenuated and spread over a large area.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A body impact protection system comprises: an inner layer including a material composition, wherein the inner layer is adjacent to a body part when the body impact protection system is worn; and an impact force dampening and defusing structure is juxtaposed to the inner layer, wherein the impart force dampening and defusing structure includes a plurality of components arranged to reduce pressure on the body part when an impact force strikes the body impact protection system by reducing a body part impact force resulting from the impact force and by increasing a body part impact area with respect to a body impact protection system impact area receiving the impact force. 2. The body impact protection system of claim 1 further comprises: an outer layer including a second material composition, wherein the impact force dampening and defusing structure is positioned between the inner layer and the outer layer. 3. The body impact protection system of claim 2 further comprises: the second material composition being substantially equal to the material composition. 4. The body impact protection system of claim 1, wherein the material composition comprises one or more of: a rubber material; a foam material; a padding material; a plastic material; a gel material; a carbon fiber material; a cloth material; a polyester material; 

 a moisture absorbing material; a moisture wicking material; and a silicon material. 5. The body impact protection system of claim 1 further comprises: the plurality of components of the impact force dampening and defusing structure is arranged to create a collision angle between the impact force and the body part thereby dividing the impact force into a normal force component and a tangential force component. 6. The body impact protection system of claim 1, wherein the impact force dampening and defusing structure comprises: a first layer of a first set of components of the plurality of components arranged in a first pattern; a second layer of a second set of components of the plurality of components arranged in a second pattern that is complimentary to the first pattern with respect to reducing pressure on the body part; and an intermediate layer between the first and second layers of the first and second sets of components, wherein the intermediate layer includes a second material composition. 7. The body impact protection system of claim 6, wherein the impact force dampening and defusing structure further comprises: a third layer of a third set of components of the plurality of components arranged in a third pattern that is complimentary to the second pattern with respect to reducing pressure on the body part; and a second intermediate layer between the second and third layers of the second and third sets of components. 8. The body impact protection system of claim 1, wherein a component of the plurality of components comprises: 

 a three-dimensional geometric shape including an impact receiving surface area and a body impact surface area, wherein the body impact surface area is larger than, and a distance "d" from, the impact receiving surface area to facilitate increasing the body part impact area with respect to the body impact protection system impact area receiving the impact force. 9. The body impact protection system of claim 1 further comprises: a first component of the plurality of components; a second component of the plurality of components; and a third component of the plurality of components, wherein the first component is positioned to receive at least a portion of the impact force, wherein the second and third components are positioned at an angle with respect to the first component such that, when the first component receives the at least the portion of the impact force, the first component collides with the second and third components to produce a plurality of multi-dimensional collisions. 10. A helmet compri ses : an outer layer including a first material composition and having a geometric shape to form an exterior surface of the helmet; an inner layer including a second material composition, wherein the inner layer is adjacent to a head when the helmet is worn; and an impact force dampening and defusing structure positioned between the inner layer and the outer layer, wherein the impart force dampening and defusing structure includes a plurality of components arranged to reduce pressure on the head when an impact force strikes the helmet by reducing a body part impact force resulting from the impact force and by increasing a body part impact area with respect to a helmet impact area receiving the impact force. 11. The helmet of claim 10 further comprises: the second material composition being substantially equal to the first material composition. 12. The helmet of claim 10, wherein each of the first and second material compositions comprises one or more of: a rubber material; a foam material; a padding material; a plastic material; a gel material; a carbon fiber material; a cloth material; a polyester material; a moisture absorbing material; a moisture wicking material; and a silicon material. 13. The helmet of claim 10 further comprises: the plurality of components of the impact force dampening and defusing structure is arranged to create a collision angle between the impact force and the head thereby dividing the impact force into a normal force component and a tangential force component. 14. The helmet of claim 10, wherein the impact force dampening and defusing structure comprises: a first layer of a first set of components of the plurality of components arranged in a first pattern; a second layer of a second set of components of the plurality of components arranged in a second pattern that is complimentary to the first pattern with respect to reducing pressure on the head; and 

 an intermediate layer between the first and second layers of the first and second sets of components, wherein the intermediate layer includes a second material composition. 15. The helmet of claim 14, wherein the impact force dampening and defusing structure further comprises: a third layer of a third set of components of the plurality of components arranged in a third pattern that is complimentary to the second pattern with respect to reducing pressure on the head; and a second intermediate layer between the second and third layers of the second and third sets of components. 16. The helmet of claim 10, wherein a component of the plurality of components comprises: a three-dimensional geometric shape including an impact receiving surface area and a body impact surface area, wherein the body impact surface area is larger than, and a distance "d" from, the impact receiving surface area to facilitate increasing the body part impact area with respect to the helmet impact area receiving the impact force. 17. The helmet of claim 10 further comprises: a first component of the plurality of components; a second component of the plurality of components; and a third component of the plurality of components, wherein the first component is positioned to receive at least a portion of the impact force, wherein the second and third components are positioned at an angle with respect to the first component such that, when the first component receives the at least the portion of the impact force, the first component collides with the second and third components to produce a plurality of multi-dimensional collisions. 18. A chest protection system comprises: an outer layer including a first material composition and having a geometric shape corresponding to a front torso of a human being; an inner layer including a second material composition, wherein the inner layer is adjacent to the front torso when the chest protection system is worn; and an impact force dampening and defusing structure positioned between the inner layer and the outer layer, wherein the impart force dampening and defusing structure includes a plurality of components arranged to reduce pressure on the front torso when an impact force strikes the chest protection system by reducing a body part impact force resulting from the impact force and by increasing a body part impact area with respect to a chest protection system impact area receiving the impact force. 19. The chest protection system of claim 18 further comprises: the second material composition being substantially equal to the first material composition. 20. The chest protection system of claim 18, wherein each of the first and second material compositions comprises one or more of: a rubber material; a foam material; a padding material; a plastic material; a gel material; a carbon fiber material; a cloth material; a polyester material; a moisture absorbing material; a moisture wicking material; and a silicon material. 21. The chest protection system of claim 18 further comprises: the plurality of components of the impact force dampening and defusing structure is arranged to create a collision angle between the impact force and the front torso thereby dividing the impact force into a normal force component and a tangential force component. 22. The chest protection system of claim 18, wherein the impact force dampening and defusing structure comprises: a first layer of a first set of components of the plurality of components arranged in a first pattern; a second layer of a second set of components of the plurality of components arranged in a second pattern that is complimentary to the first pattern with respect to reducing pressure on the front torso; and an intermediate layer between the first and second layers of the first and second sets of components, wherein the intermediate layer includes a second material composition. 23. The chest protection system of claim 22, wherein the impact force dampening and defusing structure further comprises: a third layer of a third set of components of the plurality of components arranged in a third pattern that is complimentary to the second pattern with respect to reducing pressure on the front torso; and a second intermediate layer between the second and third layers of the second and third sets of components. 24. The chest protection system of claim 18, wherein a component of the plurality of components comprises: a three-dimensional geometric shape including an impact receiving surface area and a body impact surface area, wherein the body impact surface area is larger than, and a distance "d" from, the 

 impact receiving surface area to facilitate increasing the body part impact area with respect to the body impact protection system impact area receiving the impact force. 25. The chest protection system of claim 18 further comprises: a first component of the plurality of components; a second component of the plurality of components; and a third component of the plurality of components, wherein the first component is positioned to receive at least a portion of the impact force, wherein the second and third components are positioned at an angle with respect to the first component such that, when the first component receives the at least the portion of the impact force, the first component collides with the second and third components to produce a plurality of multi-dimensional collisions.
